supports: headline cosine and compression numbers across 3 Qwen sizes
caveats: README headline (0.9485 vs 0.9226, 5.95x vs 5.02x) is correct for Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct comparing TQ_3bit vs SQ_noQJL_v3 — but the two configs are at DIFFERENT avg_bits (TQ uses 3.19 bits, SQ uses 2.69 bits). The comparison is honest only if framed as 'at matched-or-better compression ratio'.

supports: key vs value d_eff asymmetry across 5 models
caveats: gemma-2-9b-it row is an HF 403 gated-repo error, not a measurement (V1-GAP-011).
These d_eff values use NORMALIZED keys (per the v1 method) and are not comparable to the unnormalized 35.19 number in V1-RESULT-008 (V1-GAP-004).

Numbers are bound to Qwen2.5-1.5B-Instruct only. README's 2.2x latency claim is therefore single-model, not 'across architectures'.

All three methods report identical PPL to 13+ digits, which is suspicious for a real compression pipeline. Likely a too-short / non-decoding eval — must be re-run before any 'compression-neutral PPL' claim (V1-GAP-014).
Inspect ctx-length list and methods before reuse; v1 evidence map flags only the 7B headline value.

Filename and README say 10-seed; on-disk JSON contains 5 seeds (V1-GAP-001). The Wilcoxon p=0.03125 is exactly the minimum p-value achievable with n=5 paired observations and is not robust to a single dropped seed.

The two JSONs cannot both be the published latency story; v2 must reconcile or demote.

gaps.summary: Headline 10-seed CI (README, paper) but only 5 seeds on disk in results/neurips/neurips_10seed.json.
blocks_v2_claims: Wilcoxon p=0.031 cited as evidence of significance
resolution_options: Re-run 5 missing seeds.
Demote to '5 seeds' in v2 report and recompute statistics with the smaller n.
Exclude statistical-significance claim from v2 headlines.

gaps.summary: README cites 4 models in main results table; results/memory_efficiency/all_models.json contains 3 Qwen sizes only.
blocks_v2_claims: '4 models' headline in any v2 reuse
resolution_options: Restrict v2 reuse to the three Qwen rows actually present.
Re-run a 4th model and add it to the file before reuse.

gaps.summary: results/comparison/comparison_results.json shows SQ catastrophically losing to TQ at 3 bits on Qwen 1.5B (mean_improvement = -55 pp, win_rate = 0/16). Conflicts with the headline SQ-wins-on-every-head story.
blocks_v2_claims: 'SQ wins per-head' until reconciled with results/comparison/proper_comparison.json
resolution_options: Document why this configuration loses and exclude it from the headline.
If it represents a broken intermediate build, delete it or move it under results/_archive/.

gaps.summary: Latency conflict: results/neurips/neurips_latency_crossover.json reports SQ FASTER than TQ at 512 tokens (per-step 0.257 ms vs 0.566 ms); results/v3/v3_latency.json reports SQ ~50x SLOWER for compression at the same sequence length (21.04 ms vs 0.40 ms).
blocks_v2_claims: 'SQ faster than TQ at 512 tokens' headline
resolution_options: Pick one canonical latency methodology and re-run.
Demote latency from headline until reconciled.

gaps.summary: Two d_eff conventions live in the repo. Normalized-keys convention gives mean d_eff ≈ 3.95–4.30 across 5 models (V1-RESULT-002). Unnormalized eigenspectral phase 1 gives mean d_eff ≈ 35 (V1-RESULT-008). Both quoted in different places.
blocks_v2_claims: Any 'd_eff/head_dim ≈ 3–4%' claim that does not specify which convention.
resolution_options: v2 spec section 9.1 mandates normalized keys for d_eff. v2 must label all unnormalized numbers as such or remove them.

gaps.summary: results/v3/v3_perplexity_v2.json reports identical PPL = 9.5099805... to 13 digits for fp16, TQ, and SQ on Qwen 1.5B over 1023 tokens. The exact equality is suspicious for any real compression pipeline.
resolution_options: Re-run with longer context, real decoding, and per-step PPL deltas before reusing this number.

gaps.summary: results/calibration_stability/stability.json self-declares 'Reconstructed from B200 experiment log output (original file was truncated during download)'.
resolution_options: Re-run calibration stability and overwrite the reconstructed file.
Mark the existing file as derived in the v2 evidence catalog (done).

blocks_v2_claims: Any direct citation of phase 1 d_eff numbers without re-running.
resolution_options: Re-run phase 1 with gate fixed.
Retire phase 1 artifact in favor of the per-model normalized d_eff in V1-RESULT-002.

gaps.summary: README says '15-second calibration'; closest on-disk timing is wall_time_s=31.05 in V1-RESULT-009.
resolution_options: Drop the '15-second' framing.
Re-time calibration on a documented hardware target and update README from JSON.

gaps.summary: LongBench evidence (V1-RESULT-020) is n=5 per task — too small to support generalization.
resolution_options: Re-run with n ≥ 50 per task before any LongBench claim (per spec §13.7).

gaps.summary: v1 evidence map flags NIAH artifacts as limited / partly broken.
resolution_options: Treat NIAH as background until re-run with v2 spec §13.7 protocol.

gaps.summary: Two SpectralQuant engine surfaces coexist (src/spectralquant/engine.py and src/spectralquant/spectralquant.py).
blocks_v2_claims: Reproducibility — depending on import path, callers may exercise different code.
resolution_options: Pick one canonical engine in Phase 5; deprecate or alias the other; add an explicit __init__.py export.

gaps.summary: Gemma row in V1-RESULT-002 is an HF 403 gated-repo error, not a measurement.
blocks_v2_claims: 'Gemma 2-9B' as a measured architecture in v1.
resolution_options: Acquire HF access and re-run.
Drop Gemma from the architecture list until re-run.

gaps.summary: v1 evidence map says local TurboQuant baseline reproduction is marked failed in at least one phase 0 artifact.
resolution_options: Per spec §6 and §18, v2 may only claim 'beats LOCAL TurboQuant baseline' until the official Google implementation is run.

gaps.summary: Ablation files (results/comparison/ablation_v2.json, full_ablation.json) report TQ baselines that don't always agree with V1-RESULT-001 numerically.
blocks_v2_claims: Any ablation row reused as a TQ baseline without reconciliation.
resolution_options: Pick one canonical TQ baseline file per (model, bits) and reference only that ID in v2 figures.

gaps.summary: Compression-ratio formula in spec §10 does not yield 5.95x at b=3, d=3 with the simple appendix derivation. v1 README quotes 5.95x without a derivation that matches the stored bits.
blocks_v2_claims: Any compression-ratio number not produced by src/spectralquant/accounting.py.
resolution_options: Implement accounting.py per spec §10.
Refuse to print headline ratios in v2 figures unless they come from accounting.py output.
